_comment: SANITIZATION CHECKLIST (R1.2, #669) — when capturing a new fixture from a real github.copilot-chat install: STRIP request.message.* (user prompt text + parts), request.response[] (assistant markdown + tool calls), request.variableData (file paths + selected file contents), request.contentReferences[], request.codeCitations[], request.modeInfo.modeInstructions (local file URI + agent prompt), inputState.attachments[].innerText / attributes / computedStyles / ancestors (full DOM dump of the user's selection), and result.metadata.renderedUserMessage / renderedGlobalContext / codeBlocks (full prompt copy + code excerpts). KEEP envelope keys (kind, k, v shape), requestId, timestamp, timeSpentWaiting, agent.* (publisher / slashCommands metadata is GitHub-Copilot boilerplate, not user content), modelId, responseId, modelState, completionTokens / promptTokens patches, resolvedModel, and the inputState.attachments item KEY SET (ancestors / attributes / computedStyles / dimensions / fullName / icon / id / innerText / kind / modelDescription / name / value) so the parser's shape coverage stays exercised against real on-disk envelopes. Output must be shape-faithful, not content-faithful. v5 (8.5.1, #791) addendum: this fixture pins the new inputState.attachments mutation shape (DOM-like UI introspection records persisted by the chat extension for any selection the user makes against a non-source surface — Settings UI, Outline, file tree). The reducer must apply these kind:1 mutations to state.inputState.attachments WITHOUT logging unknown-shape warnings AND without producing rows from them — they are UI state, not request data. Paired with an ADR-0092 §2.3 v5 amendment that documents inputState.attachments[] as a no-emit mutation path.
_source: VS Code 1.119+ + github.copilot-chat 0.47.0, sanitized for ADR-0092 §2.3 v5

message.text: synthetic v5 user prompt for fixture only
